frameworks.name: evidence accumulation model
frameworks.one_line: accumulated evidence framework extended from choice to prospective difficulty judgment

frameworks.name: perceptual decision making
frameworks.one_line: two-alternative color dominance judgment over dynamic random-dot patches

frameworks.name: metacognition of prospective judgment
frameworks.one_line: judging task difficulty before committing to the task itself

core_question: How do observers judge which of two perceptual tasks will be easier without performing them?
key_assumptions: difficulty is operationalized as |p_blue − 0.5| of stimulus coherence
observers can accumulate evidence in parallel from both patches
a useful difficulty judgment must not require completing the task
manipulations: stimulus coherence p_blue varied across trials and across the two patches
absolute difference in stimulus difficulty between the two patches
pairings where both patches share the same difficulty level
key_findings: difficulty-judgment accuracy improved with the difference in stimulus difficulties
reaction times were not determined solely by the difficulty difference
with matched difficulty, reaction times were shorter for easier stimuli
data favored a model comparing absolute accumulated evidence with a fixed termination gap
interpretations: evidence accumulation generalizes from choice, RT and confidence to prospective difficulty judgments
a fixed-difference termination rule on absolute accumulated evidence underlies difficulty comparisons
